title: In EoE the people could come back
display_title: In EoE the people could come back
wikitext:
[]: In EoE, the people could come back?             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Tue Jul 06, 2004 10:13 am So, I was just watching The End of Evangelion. In the end of the film, I think Rei or Yui says that just so long as you can image your own form, you'll be able to live again. So, am I right in thinking that all the people in EoE that died could come back if they wanted to?

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Tue Jul 06, 2004 10:19 am They way the film ended with Yui's statement when Shinji asked if everyone will die then, yes those who had the will to comeback would then. The others would either simply die and maybe cease to eixst. Or maybe lived on through Instrumentality even though Shinji interrupted it, so I think that rules out that theory. My question is if those who were killed before Third Impact, like Misato, if they could come back to life. Im guessing since all the remains of their dead bodies turned to LCL, and it seemed Misato's soul still existed, maybe she could comeback. But what about people who died way before Third Impact like Kaji, now thats a even harder question.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Loves Toji » Tue Jul 06, 2004 6:24 pm well i think Kaji could come back, because if you think about ti Asuka died before the thrid impact was intiated. but then again, she had the will to live as yui stated, it seemed like Kaji accepted his death

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Tue Jul 06, 2004 8:12 pm The Human Instrumentality Project was meant to merge all of humanity's souls into one perfect being, that is to say, all of humanity. Though, it is never stated what "all of humanity" meant, but I would think that this includes the already deceased as well, which is an explanation for the green crosses that appeared in the EoE. My reason for thinking this is from the religous side of Eva. In the end of days the dead are supposed to be ressurected, reuniting body and soul, and the cross represents ressurection. Another mystery surrounding the deaths of those beforre third impact is Rei's (fully clothed, and luminescent, so obviously not the real Rei) presence at both Misato's and Ritsuko's deaths, a theory presented is that Rei is actually "collecting" their souls so they will be able to return. Oddly enough, this vision was absent from Asuka's death, and yet she also returned. Perhaps because she was in her Eva, possibly being protected by her mother. The question this presents is why would Rei (probably Rei II) need to appear at Misato and Ritsuko's deaths? Either my above theory is incorrect, or Rei had other reasons for appearing.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Jul 07, 2004 3:30 pm I like the the thought about the green crosses, but as for Asuka, her comming back may have more to do with Shinji than Rei. Or you could explain it away as a little plot hole, which EoE has a few of

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jul 08, 2004 2:18 am there is a fic that the people can come back in...(higher learning) however, since Shinji had the power to decide the fate of mankind maybe it was just him and whoever he wanted to take with him...

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Jul 08, 2004 1:40 pm I personally believe what Yui said, that those who can imagine themselves within their own hearts can return to life. If this is true though, can Rei come back to life?

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Thu Jul 08, 2004 3:06 pm I thought Rei died in the end because Instrumentality failed and she kind of sacrificed herself to give Shinji his wish.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Thu Jul 08, 2004 6:40 pm Asuka Loves Toji wrote: well i think Kaji could come back, because if you think about ti Asuka died before the thrid impact was intiated. but then again, she had the will to live as yui stated, it seemed like Kaji accepted his death

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Thu Jul 08, 2004 7:45 pm Hunter21 wrote: I like the the thought about the green crosses, but as for Asuka, her comming back may have more to do with Shinji than Rei. Or you could explain it away as a little plot hole, which EoE has a few of

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Thu Jul 08, 2004 7:52 pm But Rei didn't collect his soul. Or maybe she or Lilith were collecting souls since the beginning.......That would explain Rei's mysterious appearance in Episode 1.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Jul 08, 2004 8:57 pm sadsadshinji wrote: there is a fic that the people can come back in...(higher learning) however, since Shinji had the power to decide the fate of mankind maybe it was just him and whoever he wanted to take with him...

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Jul 09, 2004 5:05 am oh...ooops... wait...but he still gets to determine the course of instrumentality...

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Fri Jul 09, 2004 5:58 am cain luppus wrote: But Rei didn't collect his soul. Or maybe she or Lilith were collecting souls since the beginning.......That would explain Rei's mysterious appearance in Episode 1.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Fri Jul 09, 2004 9:25 am That wasn't in my mind at the time when I was I writing that post. I think she had been collecting souls since she was made. Or maybe since she was a clone of Lilith, then maybe Lilith collected souls back into the Black Moon since the beginning. Its an interesting theory I'd like to beleive. I think its a very flexible theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Fri Jul 09, 2004 11:00 am VERY NICE THEORY !!! :shock: I mean, we don't know what people see just before they die. Maybe all humanity since the beggining of time have seen lilith just before their deaths, but we only know that at the EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Fri Jul 09, 2004 12:28 pm Thanks! The thing is why did Rei appear to Shinji if he wasn't ready to die? Either she was collecting souls from Sachiels rampage and sensed Shinji and maybe somehow Shinji had this aurora that alerted Rei, seeing that he does save humanity kinda. Or maybe it represents a/the loop hole in the circle of life. She appeared in the beginning (of the series),=birth and appears at the EoE =death.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Fri Jul 09, 2004 1:24 pm Some theology states that the moon is white because it is full of souls, so yeah, I think I like that theory. :)

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Fri Jul 09, 2004 2:57 pm I think we might be on to somthing here.....

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Fri Jul 09, 2004 4:42 pm :shock: Wow, I suprised myself with this one. Hehe.....
